component.name: Bottom Sheet: Choose Job
component.description: Second step after quick capture: back affordance, contextual Title-H3, full-width accent quick-action row (icon well + two lines), LABEL divider “or attach to existing job”, then a stack of simple job row cards.

captureAxis.note: Featured row subtitle is session-style (“Begin tracking…”) only for `startSession`; all other kinds use inbox copy (“Save to inbox…”).

quickIcons.note: Reuses `QuickCaptureTileIcon` from Quick Capture sheet for the 40px accent well.

figmaLayerNames.componentSet: Bottom Sheet: Choose Job (vertical stack of variant masters, 16px gap)
figmaLayerNames.handleWrap: choose-job-sheet-handle-wrap (TSX only; Figma uses handle bar under surface)
figmaLayerNames.header: choose-job-sheet-header (sheet title text; back button is a sibling frame)
figmaLayerNames.title: choose-job-sheet-title
figmaLayerNames.jobList: choose-job-sheet-job-list (featured Row Card, divider, simple job Row Cards)

codeProps.captureKind: ActionTileKind — drives sheet title, accent fill, quick-action copy, and icon.
codeProps.jobs: Array of { id, title, subtitle } for simple job rows.

notes: 2026-04-07: Figma frame name restored to “Bottom Sheet: Choose Job”. Code: `ChooseJobBottomSheet` in `bottom-sheet-choose-job/`.
2026-04-10: Added kebab-case structural hooks for the sheet wrapper, handle, header, quick-action row, divider, and job list.
Material variant uses `Semantic/Status/Success/Text` for the featured row fill in Figma (`1075:2030`); other non-session kinds use activity / info tokens per file.
findings: 2026-04-08: Component set `1075:1948` — `VERTICAL` + `NO_WRAP`, 16px `itemSpacing`; `clipsContent` false on set and variant masters. Session master (`1075:1947`) surface corrected to `choose-job-sheet` (was mislabeled `choose-job-sheet-content`); `choose-job-sheet-body` → `choose-job-sheet-content`. All six variants: `Card Rows` → `choose-job-sheet-job-list`; `Heading` / `choose-job-sheet-header-title` → `choose-job-sheet-header` + `choose-job-sheet-title`; `Back Button` → `choose-job-sheet-back-button` (+ label wrap / label / icon); featured Row Card instance → `choose-job-sheet-quick-action`; divider container → `choose-job-sheet-divider` (+ line-left/right, label wrap, label). React keeps a single `choose-job-sheet-header` wrapper with back + title; Figma keeps title header frame and back as siblings under content. No `flatten()` in automation.
